text: I feel a curious sense of anticipation. Standing in this bright, industrial corridor, peering into a glass-walled room filled with glowing screens and vibrant colors, I am drawn forward. The display, marked R+D, promises a dialogue between past innovations and future possibilities. I feel like a participant in a living experiment—part observer, part experimenter—aware of the weight of progress and the ceaseless motion of discovery. There is an undercurrent of excitement, but also humility: here, I am but one mind, seeking connection and insight among many, as technology and history converge around me.
